channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria Charges Six With Treason Over Alleged Coup Plot
item.description: Nigerian authorities say they foiled a plot to overthrow President Bola Tinubu and charged six people including a retired major general and a serving police inspector with terrorism and treason.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South Africa Police Chief Charged in $21 Million Corruption Scandal
item.description: National police commissioner Fannie Masemola faces charges over an unlawful contract, marking a major blow to law enforcement leadership in one of Africa's largest economies.

item.title: ⛪ Trump and Top Republicans Join Marathon Bible Reading Event
item.description: President Donald Trump and conservative Christian supporters are participating in a marathon Bible reading event, highlighting the administration's outreach to evangelical voters.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Court Rules Hungary LGBTQ Law Violates EU Law
item.description: The European Court of Justice found that Hungary's legislation banning LGBTQ content for minors violates EU law, in a landmark ruling with implications for rule-of-law disputes.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Bloomberg: Can China Escape the Japanification Trap?
item.description: As China's economy slows, analysts debate whether Beijing can avoid the decades-long stagnation that plagued Japan, with implications for global growth.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping Calls for Strait of Hormuz to Reopen
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman that the strategic waterway should remain open, reflecting Beijing's difficult balancing act between Iran and Gulf states during the conflict.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Issues Megaquake Advisory After 7.7 Magnitude Earthquake
item.description: A powerful earthquake off northern Japan triggered tsunami alerts and a rare advisory warning of slightly elevated megaquake risk. Officials urged preparedness in 182 coastal towns.

item.title: 🇹🇩 Chad Deploys 1,500 Troops to Haiti for UN Gang Suppression Force
item.description: Chad will send two battalions to join the Kenya-led multinational force combating gang violence in Haiti, where armed groups control most of Port-au-Prince.

item.title: 🍎 Tim Cook Steps Down as Apple CEO, Hardware Chief John Ternus to Succeed Him
item.description: After 15 years leading Apple, Tim Cook will become executive chairman on Sept. 1. Hardware engineering head John Ternus takes over as CEO as the company navigates the AI transition.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇰🇷 India and South Korea Pledge to Double Trade to $50 Billion by 2030
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung met PM Modi in New Delhi to strengthen supply chains amid Iran war disruptions. Focus on shipbuilding, defense, AI, and critical minerals.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese Carrier Liaoning Transits Taiwan Strait for First Time Since December
item.description: The Chinese aircraft carrier Liaoning sailed through the Taiwan Strait in a move that comes days after a Japanese destroyer made a similar passage on a historically sensitive date for China.

item.title: ⚡ IEA Chief: Iran War Creating Worst Energy Crisis in History
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency says the US-Iran conflict has created the biggest energy crisis ever, surpassing the combined effects of the 1973, 1979, and 2022 crises when combined with Russian gas supply disruptions.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Sees Growing Interest in Orlan Drones Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Russian drone manufacturer STC reports increased foreign interest in Orlan-series UAVs at the DSA exhibition in Malaysia, citing the Middle East conflict and demand for cost-effective surveillance systems.

item.title: 🤖 Palantir Faces Backlash Over AI-Driven Military Doctrine
item.description: The defense tech company sparked debate with a social media thread promoting AI-driven military deterrence, arguing Silicon Valley has an obligation to support national defense and that AI will define the next era of warfare.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Markets on Edge as Trump Signals No Ceasefire Extension Without Deal
item.description: President Trump told Bloomberg the US-Iran ceasefire is highly unlikely to be extended without an agreement, adding the naval blockade outside the Strait of Hormuz will remain until a deal is signed.

item.title: 🇲🇲 Myanmar Junta Leader Offers Peace Talks, Rebel Groups Reject
item.description: Myanmar's new military-backed president Min Aung Hlaing proposed peace talks with opposition groups within 100 days, but the Karen National Union and Chin National Front rejected the offer, saying they have no plans to negotiate with an illegitimate administration.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Visits Cameroon to Address Separatist Conflict
item.description: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Cameroon for a four-nation Africa tour, with separatists declaring a three-day ceasefire to allow safe passage. The visit highlights one of the world's most neglected conflicts in the country's Anglophone regions, where thousands have died since 2017.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuela Opposition Leader Machado Plans Return, Urges Elections
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize winner Maria Corina Machado said she expects to return to Venezuela by end of 2026 and is pressing for swift elections. She warned that delays risk civil unrest as Venezuelans expect major changes after Maduro's capture.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Scraps Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Defense Shift
item.description: Japan has overturned decades of postwar pacifist policy by lifting restrictions on overseas arms sales, opening the door for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons as it seeks to counter regional security threats.

item.title: 🏛️ UN Secretary-General Candidates Face Live Public Grillings in New York
item.description: Four candidates vying to become the next UN secretary-general face televised hearings Tuesday and Wednesday, marking a rare public scrutiny process for the leadership of the crisis-wracked global organization.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Pushes for Iran Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: Security remains tight in Pakistan ahead of hoped-for talks between US and Iranian delegations as a two-week ceasefire set to expire Wednesday, with Vance and top officials expected to arrive in Islamabad.

item.title: 🇯🇵⚔️ Japan Overhauls Pacifist Defense Policy to Enter Global Arms Market
item.description: The Japanese government has scrapped restrictions on overseas arms sales in a major shift from post-WWII pacifist policies, seeking to counter regional security threats from China and North Korea.

item.title: 🇮🇷🚢 Iran Demands Release of Seized Vessel as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
item.description: Vice President JD Vance and top US officials are expected to depart for Islamabad ahead of potential peace talks, while Iran files UN complaint over US seizure of cargo vessel Touska.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran grants field commanders greater autonomy over Iraq militias amid war pressures
item.description: Iran has given its commanders more power to direct militia operations in Iraq without central approval, a decentralization driven by the ongoing conflict with the US. This shift could lead to more unpredictable attacks and complicates diplomatic efforts.

item.title: 🚢 Seized Iranian ship Touska carried dual-use equipment, US sources say
item.description: The Iranian-flagged container ship seized by US forces on Sunday was likely carrying equipment that could have both civilian and military applications. The seizure highlights continued enforcement of the US naval blockade despite ceasefire negotiations.
